title: Catanthera kinabaluensis
intro: Catanthera kinabaluensis is a rare, endangered species of flowering plant in the Gentianaceae family, native to the mountainous regions of Borneo. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. Bioactive constituents of Catanthera kinabaluensis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Catanthera kinabaluensis include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are used to treat conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections.
